title: Measuring Harness-Induced Belief Divergence in Multi-Step LLM Agents
acquisition_purpose: Primary-source acquisition for review-harness-belief-divergence-validity; this record is not a deep review or release audit.

paper.withdrawal_status: The immutable v1 API entry contains a normal abstract and no withdrawal or retraction notice.
paper.extraction_status: The complete immutable 28-page v1 PDF extracted successfully. The title, abstract, main manuscript, controlled and public-benchmark results, BIWM component and alignment sections, metric definitions, harness specifications, reproducibility notes, and final appendix table were inspected for readable continuity.

official_repository.identity_evidence: The paper explicitly links this repository as its code release, and the repository README identifies the same paper and authors. This acquisition record does not establish exact paper-time artifact identity or validate released results.
official_repository.license: No license is declared by the GitHub repository API and no license file appears in the acquired 56-file archive; reuse rights are therefore not inferred.
official_repository.timing_boundary: The repository predates the arXiv v1 submission, but the requested pinned commit is dated 10 days 8 hours 55 minutes 21 seconds after the v1 submission timestamp. It is acquisition-time official release evidence, not demonstrated byte-identical paper-time code.

acquisition_scope: Complete immutable-v1 paper/PDF/text plus the requested commit-pinned official repository snapshot and GitHub metadata. The archive was integrity-tested and its 56-file inventory was inspected, but the release was not deeply audited, replayed, or subjected to zero-call tests in this acquisition task.
claim_boundary: This evidence supports later full-paper reading and static/executable release audit. Acquisition alone does not establish that elicited structured rollouts measure latent belief, that reported divergence is robust to encoding or weighting, that harness effects causally mediate actions or outcomes, that retained artifacts reproduce paper tables, or that the method has professional, production, safety, validity, or readiness evidence.
